devdoc.details: All balances are stored as encrypted euint64 values  Key Features: - Deposits are encrypted and added to user balances - Withdrawals use encrypted comparisons to prevent overdrafts - Only authorized addresses can decrypt their own balance - Agent operators can read balances to verify execution

balanceOf(address).details: Returns the ciphertext handle - cannot be read without decryption

returns._0: The encrypted balance as a euint64 handle

params._agentOperator: Address that can read encrypted balances

deposit(bytes32,bytes,address).details: Anyone can call this to deposit for any user (agent deposits on behalf of users)

params.user: The user whose balance should be credited

getBalanceHandle(address).details: Frontend uses this with relayer SDK to decrypt

returns._0: handle The raw bytes32 handle of the encrypted balance
True if user has a balance entry

makeBalanceDecryptable(address).details: This is a convenience function for the frontend User must call this once per session to decrypt their balance

updateAgentOperator(address).details: Only current operator can update (for security during demo)

withdraw(bytes32,bytes).details: Uses encrypted comparison to prevent overdrafts without revealing balance

devdoc.title: EncryptedVault

SenderNotAllowedToUseHandle(bytes32,addr.notice: Returned if the sender is not allowed to use the handle.

ZamaProtocolUnsupported().notice: Returned if the Zama protocol is not supported on the current chain

balanceOf(address).notice: Get encrypted balance for a user

constructor.notice: Constructor sets the initial agent operator

getBalanceHandle(address).notice: Get the raw encrypted balance handle for client-side decryption

hasBalance(address).notice: Check if a balance is initialized for a user

makeBalanceDecryptable(address).notice: Emergency function to allow a user to make their balance decryptable

userdoc.notice: Confidential DeFi savings vault using Zama fhEVM
